bull_thesis: MYRG is a pure-play physical contractor for the two most bottlenecked layers of the AI build-out: high-voltage grid interconnect (T&D) and mission-critical data-center electrical fit-out (C&I). The fundamental trajectory is unambiguously strengthening: net income went $30.3M (FY24) -> $118.4M (FY25) -> $46.8M in Q1-26 alone; EPS $1.83 -> $7.53 -> $2.99/qtr; gross margin 8.6% -> 11.6% -> 13.4%; record $2.84B backlog. In Q1-26 management RAISED both the FY revenue guide (10%->12%) and BOTH segment operating-margin bands (T&D to 8-11%, C&I to 6-9%) -- a double raise, not a beat-and-hold. The demand is quantified and structural: 'Data center construction starts are up nearly 100% year-over-year' (Egan), large transmission awards (345kV SC, AZ/TX substations) booking revenue 'in 2027 or later,' and the CEO framing T&D as a decade-long nationwide build vs the localized 2013-15 CREZ boom. The balance sheet is pristine -- $163M cash, $9M funded debt, 0.04x leverage, $230M LTM FCF -- giving real optionality for M&A ('high-quality companies out there') or buybacks. ROE ~22.7%, ROIC ~15.5% above WACC and rising. MSA work is now ~70% of T&D revenue, improving recurrence/quality-of-earnings. Management is disciplined ('selective on larger projects,' 'don't want to be first-in') and pre-discloses friction (DSO drift). This is the picks-and-shovels electrical contractor that EVERY grid + data-center capex dollar must pay, run by a credible, improving team with a fortress balance sheet. Conditions: AI/electrification capex stays durable; large-transmission awards convert to backlog in 2H-26/2027; margins hold near the raised guide bands.
bear_thesis: The business is excellent; the STOCK is the problem. MYRG is +176% in 52 weeks to ~$461, trading at ~50x trailing P/E, ~39x forward, ~27x EV/EBITDA -- ABOVE the analyst median price target (~$304) and at the TOP of the entire contractor cohort (PWR ~25x, MTZ ~26x, PRIM ~16x EV/EBITDA). The market has fully priced a flawless multi-year build-out; the marginal surprise now has to be even-better-than-record to drive further gains. The first crack is already visible: book-to-bill is running BELOW 1.0x on a trailing basis (backlog +7.7% YoY vs revenue +20% YoY) -- the order book is growing slower than revenue is being burned, and management's defense is 'large projects come in future quarters,' a promise not a print. Q1's headline 13.4% gross margin is FLATTERED by close-outs and favorable change orders (management's own words: margins are 'lumpy'); the durable run-rate is the lower guide bands. The moat is NARROW -- electrical construction is fragmented, low-switching-cost, re-bid project-by-project; new data-center entrants are appearing (margin pressure 'not yet' visible). And the thesis has NO idiosyncratic resolution driver -- it rests entirely on the SAME AI-capex/electrification wave the rest of scale-out Basket B depends on, so it offers zero diversification and full correlation to any hyperscaler capex digestion. The risk is not bankruptcy or secular decline; it is a high-multiple de-rate on any growth/margin wobble or a single soft backlog print. Confirmation: a quarter where book-to-bill stays <1.0x, gross margin reverts toward the mid-teens guide, or a hyperscaler capex guide-down hits the whole cohort.
sop_bull: Whole-company intrinsic (right metric for a single-economics contractor; segments share labor/bonding/management and are not separately monetizable, so SoP = segment-margin-weighted whole-co). FY26E revenue ~$4.1B; assume EBITDA margin reaches ~7.5% on the raised guides -> ~$307M EBITDA. Bull multiple 22x EV/EBITDA (premium to PWR/MTZ ~25-26x is unwarranted; cap at a still-rich 22x for a name growing 12% with rising margins) -> EV ~$6.76B; +$154M net cash -> equity ~$6.9B / 15.57M shares ~= $443/share. Cross-check on earnings: FY26E EPS ~$9.47 x a generous 38x (peer-premium growth multiple) ~= $360; the EV/EBITDA path is more generous because it capitalizes the margin inflection. Bull case fair value: ~$443-460/share -- i.e. roughly the CURRENT price, meaning the bull case is essentially 'the stock is fairly valued for a perfect build-out,' not undervalued.
sop_bear: Whole-company intrinsic, normalized margins + de-rate. FY26E revenue ~$4.1B; normalize EBITDA margin to the DURABLE ~6.5% (strip Q1 close-out flatter) -> ~$267M EBITDA. Bear multiple 16x EV/EBITDA (PRIM-cohort level -- appropriate once the growth premium compresses on a soft backlog print or capex-digestion scare) -> EV ~$4.27B; +$154M net cash -> equity ~$4.42B / 15.57M shares ~= $284/share. Earnings cross-check: FY26E EPS ~$9.47 x 26x (PRIM-level forward) ~= $246. Bear range: ~$246-284/share -- a ~38-47% drawdown from ~$461, which lands right at the analyst median target (~$304) on the gentler path.
sop_breakdown: Base-case whole-company intrinsic: FY26E revenue ~$4.1B x durable ~7.0% EBITDA margin = ~$287M EBITDA x 18-19x EV/EBITDA (a justified premium to the PRIM-level 16x but a discount to PWR/MTZ ~25x, reflecting MYRG's smaller scale and narrower moat) = EV ~$5.3-5.45B; + $154M net cash = equity ~$5.45-5.6B / 15.57M shares = ~$350-360 on the upper bound, ~$305 on a tougher 16x; midpoint ~$330. Earnings cross-check: FY26E EPS ~$9.47 x ~34x (a premium-but-sane forward multiple for 12% growth + rising margins) = ~$322. The two methods converge on ~$320-345. Note this base case sits ~25-30% BELOW the current ~$461 price and right around the analyst median target (~$304) -- the AI-capex optionality is already MORE than priced in.
risk_reward: UNFAVORABLE at ~$461. Downside-to-base ~$330 = -28%; downside-to-bear ~$284 = -38%. Upside-to-bull ~$455 ~= 0% (the bull case is roughly the current price). Risk/reward is approximately -38% downside vs ~0% upside to a perfect-execution bull -- i.e. negatively skewed. You are paying full price for a flawless decade of build-out with a backlog (book-to-bill <1.0x) that is already whispering caution.
peer_comps_note: TRUE peers are the listed electrical/infrastructure construction contractors levered to grid + data-center capex, verified live June 2026: Quanta Services (PWR) ~25x EV/EBITDA -- the scale leader, broadest grid + renewables + DC exposure; MasTec (MTZ) ~26x EV/EBITDA, ~40x fwd P/E -- comms + power delivery, highest growth multiple; Primoris (PRIM) ~16x EV/EBITDA, ~27x fwd P/E -- the cohort VALUE name. MYRG: ~27x EV/EBITDA, ~50x trailing / ~39x fwd P/E. RANKING -- VALUATION: MYRG is the MOST expensive on EV/EBITDA (27x vs cohort 16-26x) and second-most on fwd P/E -- a meaningful de-rate risk. GROWTH: MYRG ~12% revenue / ~26% EPS guide is solid but below MTZ; mid-pack. MARGINS: MYRG's recovered 13.4% gross / ~7% EBITDA is good and improving but structurally thinner than PWR's scale margins; the inflection (not the level) is the story. RETURNS: ROIC ~15.5% / ROE ~22.7% are strong and cohort-competitive. AI-CAPEX EXPOSURE: MYRG is a focused, high-purity play (DC starts +100%, but DC only ~6% of revenue today; the bulk is grid T&D which is the same scale-out wave) -- comparable purity to PWR, less diversified than MTZ. NET: MYRG ranks BEST-or-tied on returns/margin-trajectory and balance sheet, but WORST on valuation -- it is the most-expensive way to own the cheapest-moat layer of the cohort. PRIM at 16x is the value-relative alternative within the same thesis.
role_in_scaleout: MYRG is the physical electrical contractor that energizes the grid interconnects (T&D high-voltage lines, substations) and wires the mission-critical data halls (C&I) -- the literal labor that turns a hyperscaler's capex into a powered facility; demand is highly durable through the build-out decade because grid modernization + electrification underinvestment is structural and labor/material-constrained, but it is fully correlated to AI-capex durability with no idiosyncratic driver.
consensus_delta: There is NO favorable consensus delta here -- the street is NOT looking the wrong way; it is, if anything, MORE bullish than the fundamentals warrant. The stock at ~$461 trades ABOVE the analyst median price target of ~$304 and ABOVE even the high target (~$332), meaning the market has run past the sell-side's already-optimistic models. Consensus assumes: (1) the raised 12% revenue / raised-margin-band guide holds AND keeps ratcheting; (2) the close-out-flattered 13.4% gross margin is a durable new floor rather than a Q1 peak; (3) large-transmission awards convert to backlog on schedule despite trailing book-to-bill already <1.0x. The expectations-arbitrage runs AGAINST a buyer here: the mispricing is that a strong-but-cyclical contractor with a NARROW moat is priced at ~27x EV/EBITDA -- the TOP of a cohort whose value name (PRIM) trades at 16x. The 'wrong' assumption is the implicit one that MYRG deserves a premium-to-cohort multiple; on a narrower moat and smaller scale than PWR, it arguably deserves a discount. The pain trade is DOWN (de-rate), not up.
valley_of_death: No solvency or cash-burn valley -- the balance sheet is pristine (0.04x leverage, $163M cash, $230M LTM FCF), so there is no maturity wall or burn hump. The near-term (3-9mo) risk is purely MULTIPLE/EXPECTATIONS: (1) a Q2/Q3-2026 print where book-to-bill stays <1.0x or backlog growth decelerates further would crack the 'decade of visibility' narrative on a 27x multiple; (2) gross margin reverting from 13.4% toward the mid-teens-op guide as close-out tailwinds fade reads as a 'miss' even though it is just normalization; (3) the CFO-flagged DSO drift (mid-50s -> low-60s) pressures reported FCF on the MSA-mix shift; (4) the dominant systemic risk is a hyperscaler AI-capex guide-down (the regime doc names this as ~40% of 2026 S&P EPS growth and the single load-bearing wall) -- a capex scare de-grosses the ENTIRE scale-out cohort simultaneously, and MYRG at the top of the valuation cohort would fall hardest. The valley of death is a de-rate, not a default.
positioning_washout: No yield-base or dividend-cut washout dynamic -- MYRG pays no meaningful dividend and the holder base is growth/momentum, not income. The mechanical risk is the OPPOSITE: after +176% in 52 weeks, the shareholder base is crowded with momentum and AI-thematic capital that bought the breakout, not the value. That base is reflexive -- it will liquidate on the FIRST disappointing backlog/margin print or any cohort-wide AI-capex de-gross, because there is no valuation floor to catch it (the stock is already above the analyst median target). This is a momentum-unwind risk, not a forced-passive-seller catalyst, but it makes the downside path faster and more violent than the fundamentals alone imply.
forcing_function: There is no positive forcing function pending -- the re-rating catalyst (the FY24->FY25->Q1-26 margin recovery + the AI/grid demand reveal) has ALREADY FIRED and the +176% move is the re-rate. The dated events ahead are RISK-skewed: the Q2-2026 earnings call (~late-July 2026, where book-to-bill and margin-durability get re-tested) and any hyperscaler capex guidance update (the regime tripwire). The next forcing function for THIS stock is a downside one -- a soft backlog print or a cohort capex scare -- not an upside one.
moderator_conclusion: MYRG is a textbook case of a GREAT BUSINESS at a BAD PRICE, and the regime makes that worse, not better. The Interrogator (credibility 4/5, STRENGTHENING, NARROW/STABLE moat, manageable secular threat) is right that the fundamentals are excellent and corroborated: net income 4x'd off the FY24 trough, margins inflected, backlog at record, balance sheet pristine, ROIC above WACC and rising, management credible and disciplined. But the load-bearing catalyst -- the recovery + AI-grid demand reveal -- has already FIRED, and the +176%/52wk move IS the re-rate. Applying CATALYST_WATCH_REGIME.md: the regime PENALIZES already-fired / already-ran catalysts and rate-rescue theses, and demands resolution-driver INDEPENDENCE; MYRG fails both -- its catalyst is spent and its entire thesis hinges on the single dominant shared macro factor (AI-capex durability) that the regime flags as ~40% of 2026 EPS growth and the #1 book-concentration risk. At ~$461 the stock trades at ~27x EV/EBITDA (the TOP of the PWR/MTZ/PRIM cohort, vs PRIM's 16x) and ABOVE the analyst median target (~$304). My reconciled base-case SoP is ~$320-345; bear ~$284. That is ~28% downside to base, ~38% to bear, against essentially zero upside to a perfect-execution bull (~$455). Risk/reward is negatively skewed and the first hairline crack -- book-to-bill <1.0x -- is already in the data. The AI-scale-out optionality is not just priced in; it is OVER-priced in. VERDICT C / conviction 2: this is the most-expensive way to own the narrowest-moat layer of the grid cohort, with a fired catalyst and full correlation to the basket's shared macro tail. If forced to own grid-construction exposure, PRIM (16x) is the value-relative expression and PWR (scale leader) the quality-relative one; MYRG only becomes interesting on a >30% de-rate toward the high-$200s/low-$300s where the SoP and the durable-margin floor actually support the price. value_conviction is ALSO 2: stripping the catalyst/regime overlay entirely, the valuation still sits ~25-30% above intrinsic SoP fair value -- there is no value cushion, only quality at a premium. Wait for the wash-out.
interrogator_dossier:
# MYR Group (MYRG) - Forensic Dossier

*Interrogator pass - Opus 4.8 - evaluated within the AI scale-out / scale-up build-out (scale-out Basket B - GRID). Source: web (Q1-2026 earnings call transcript + Q4/FY-2025 + FY-2024 results, live fundamentals June 2026).*

MYR Group is a US/Canada electrical-construction contractor in two segments: Transmission & Distribution (T&D) and Commercial & Industrial (C&I). It is a labor-and-execution business that bids, builds, and closes out high-voltage lines, substations, and mission-critical electrical fit-outs (data centers, water/wastewater, semiconductor, healthcare). Its role in the AI build-out is the physical electrical contractor who actually energizes the grid interconnects and wires the data halls.

## 1. NARRATIVE ARC

The story over the last ~6 quarters is a margin-recovery + structural-demand-inflection arc, and it has dramatically strengthened. FY-2024 was the trough (net income $30.3M, EPS $1.83, gross margin 8.6%; defensive 'de-risk the book' framing after bad clean-energy/large-C&I projects). FY-2025 was the turn (revenue $3.66B, net income $118.4M +291%, EPS $7.53, gross margin 11.6%; pivot from 'fixing the book' to 'structural demand is here'). Q1-2026 confirmed the inflection (record revenue $1.0B +20%, net income $46.8M, EPS $2.99, gross margin 13.4%, EBITDA $81.5M, record backlog $2.84B; FY revenue guide raised 10%->12% and BOTH segment operating-margin bands raised). New drivers introduced and quantified: 'Data center construction starts are up nearly 100% year-over-year' (Egan), water/wastewater as strongest C&I grower, large transmission visibility into 2027+. CEO anchored T&D to a decade-long nationwide build vs the localized 2013-15 CREZ boom. Nothing material dropped; additions are accretive and corroborated.

## 2. CLAIMS vs FINANCIALS (forensic)

Record revenue/backlog/EBITDA: CORROBORATED (real records, not adjusted theater). Structurally higher margins: CORROBORATED but partly cyclical -- management itself flagged 'favorable change orders' and 'project closeouts,' so part of Q1's 13.4% is non-repeatable; the structural floor is the raised guide (T&D 8-11% op, C&I 6-9% op), not 13.4%. Structural multi-year demand: CORROBORATED on direction, but backlog +7.7% trails revenue +20% -> trailing book-to-bill <1.0x (yellow flag bulls gloss over). Pristine balance sheet / optionality: CORROBORATED (funded debt $9M, cash $163M, 0.04x leverage, $230M LTM FCF -- cleanest claim in the file). Strong returns: CORROBORATED (ROE ~22.7%, 3-yr ROIC ~15.5%, above WACC and rising). Net: narrative roughly matched by numbers with one honest, self-disclosed caveat (Q1 margin flattered by close-outs).

## 3. TONE & CONFIDENCE TRAJECTORY - Rising

FY-2024 defensive ('de-risk,' 'selectivity,' apologizing for inefficiencies) -> FY-2025 'recovery on track' -> Q1-2026 clearly offensive: raising guidance twice (revenue + both margin bands), CEO invoking a decade-long nationwide transmission build, COO quantifying DC starts +100%. Yet disciplined hedges retained: 'we continue to be selective on the larger projects,' 'we don't want to be the first in,' 'we haven't seen margin pressure from these new entrants.' Capex guided ABOVE historical norm (3% of revenue) -- investing into the cycle. Shift from defensive to offensive is unambiguous and evidence-backed.

## 4. GUIDANCE CREDIBILITY - Medium-High (4/5)

Short, asymmetric record: Q4-2024 was a MISS (EPS + revenue), so reliability cannot be scored 'High' on a clean beat streak. But the four-quarter recovery culminating in a RAISED FY-2026 guide (10%->12%, both margin bands up) has been delivered/exceeded each quarter through Q1-2026. CFO pre-disclosed DSO drift (mid-50s -> low-60s from MSA mix) -- a credibility GREEN flag. Held back from 5 only by the recency of the FY-24 miss and the close-out-flattered Q1 margin.

## 5. ANALYST PRESSURE POINTS

Sustainability of the 13.4% gross margin (the contested variable -- new floor or close-out peak?); book-to-bill / backlog conversion (why backlog +7.7% lags revenue +20%; management deflects to 'future quarters,' revenue '2027 or later' -- plausible but UNVERIFIED until awards book); new data-center competition (CEO's 'no margin pressure yet' is a claim to watch); labor/materials availability (management itself flagged clients worried 'where they get the material' and 'how they secure labor' -- the real governor on the build-out, two-sided).

## 6. RED FLAGS / GREEN FLAGS

GREEN: net-cash balance sheet (0.04x), $230M LTM FCF; broad-based margin recovery across both segments; ROE ~22.7%/ROIC ~15.5% above WACC and rising; management pre-disclosing DSO friction; quantified secular demand (DC +100%, decade transmission build, water). RED: valuation detached from fundamentals (+176%/52wk to ~$461; ~50x trailing / ~39x fwd P/E / ~27x EV/EBITDA -- ABOVE analyst median target ~$304 and at the TOP of the cohort vs PRIM 16x); book-to-bill <1.0x trailing (order book growing slower than revenue burned); Q1 gross margin flattered by close-outs/change orders (durable margin is the lower guide); single-factor dependence on AI-capex/electrification durability shared with the rest of Basket B.

## 7. HIDDEN SIGNALS

Terminology shift from 'de-risk the book' (2024) to 'selective, don't want to be first-in' (2026) -- same discipline now from strength (demand-rationing) not damage-control. Capex ABOVE historical norm (3% of revenue, T&D-weighted) -- believing demand durable enough to own capacity. M&A language reactivated ('high-quality companies out there'; cash for 'acquisitions...or buybacks'). MSA work now ~70% of T&D revenue -- quietly important quality-of-earnings improvement (more recurring, less lumpy-bid) at the cost of higher DSO.

## 8. CAPITAL-ALLOCATION VERDICT (for the director)

Trajectory: STRENGTHENING. The business is unambiguously improving -- margins, returns, backlog, balance sheet, demand visibility all moved the right way, corroborated by hard numbers, credible/pre-disclosing management. The single thing the director must weigh: the gap between the BUSINESS (excellent, strengthening) and the STOCK (already priced for perfection at ~50x trailing / 27x EV/EBITDA, +176%/yr, ABOVE the analyst median target and at the top of the peer cohort). The AI-capex optionality is largely ALREADY in the price; the marginal surprise must be even-stronger-than-record to drive further expansion -- and book-to-bill <1.0x is the first hairline crack. Moat: scale-cost + execution-reputation + bonding/skilled-labor intangible, but electrical construction is fragmented, low-switching-cost, re-bid project-by-project -> NARROW / STABLE (rising returns but off a recovery trough; structurally contestable; new entrants appearing). Secular threat: this is a WINNER of electrification/grid build-out, not a victim; terminal risk is CYCLICAL (capex digestion air-pocket), not structural impairment -- AI does not displace the physical act of energizing high-voltage lines. Secular threat: none-to-manageable. It is not cheap; it is expensive on a strengthening base, so the risk is a high-multiple de-rate on any growth wobble, not a value trap.

CREDIBILITY_SCORE: 4 | TRAJECTORY: STRENGTHENING | MOAT: NARROW | MOAT_TREND: STABLE | SECULAR_THREAT: manageable
